title: NERV positions and salaries
wikitext:
[]: NERV positions and salaries             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Mar 24, 2007 3:10 pm First of all, I'm posting this in the discussion forum, however depending on how the responses go it may be more appropriate in chit-chat. This topic is for the discussion of a few things: What kind of training/education is required to hold certain NERV positions? What kind of training/knowledge does Misato have that qualifies her as Operations Director? It seems Hyuga, Aoba, and Maya have quite extensive technical knowledge. What kind of pay comes from these positions? Seems Misato is paid a crappy salary for such an important job. "The cost of getting my laundry done is killing me. [dub quote omg]" WTF Why? plot device. or something.

[]: Re: NERV positions and salaries             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sat Mar 24, 2007 3:12 pm slothen wrote: Seems Misato is paid a crappy salary for such an important job.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Mar 24, 2007 3:32 pm but how does one get to be operations director? She doesn't seem to have very much useful knowledge about the Eva's so is she put there for pure convenience to the story? Does she have prior military experience relevant to... anything?

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Sat Mar 24, 2007 3:42 pm Do I recall a conversation about the city having a high cost of living? Or sm just I thinking of the housewifes 'how dangerous giant monsters are!' talk in the supermarket? but how does one get to be operations director? She doesn't seem to have very much useful knowledge about the Eva's so is she put there for pure convenience to the story? Does she have prior military experience relevant to... anything?

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Mar 24, 2007 3:44 pm Katayoku no Tenshi wrote: It seems a university education is neccassery and having a parent that was sacrificed on the dark alter of your parent organizations ambitions may be useful in some way.

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Sat Mar 24, 2007 3:54 pm Well Yui was hardly sacrificed. Maybe she applied for the job. 'Who wants to be in a position of terrifying responsibility, command and raise troubbled children as they pilot giant beings of death? . . . No takers?' 'I'll take it!' 'Great! We don't care if you have some weird revenge thing that will put the fate of mankind into question. cos we're gonna smush ya all down into a metabeing anyway. ' 'What was that?' or rather:

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Mar 24, 2007 3:58 pm haha she volunteered for that. OK i give up. Plot device is the answer to all. :x :x

[]:             Quote Post by Szmitten » Sat Mar 24, 2007 4:00 pm I was kind of under the impression that the combination of Fuyutsuki seeing Misato when she was mute and being friends with Ritsuko got a foot in the door. Also, the position could almost be seen as a consolation for having her father die, and because of her stated intents for revenge against the angels.

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Sat Mar 24, 2007 4:07 pm Or the presence of a higher than average amount of Narrativium in Nerv headquarters. Think about it anyone who is perfectly suited for a job must never/will never do that job because they will fail. Isn't that one of the laws of anime? Or maybe she's the operations director because she's unsuited for the job? As everyone in Evangelion is (how the fusk did Gendo get his job?) but as stated above she still succeeds because she must. Or maybe just maybe she's the character that was chosen to be the operations director so she became it. She certainly wasn't in the military.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Mar 24, 2007 4:59 pm Think about it anyone who is perfectly suited for a job must never/will never do that job because they will fail. Isn't that one of the laws of anime?

[]: Re: NERV positions and salaries             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Sat Mar 24, 2007 4:59 pm slothen wrote: Seems Misato is paid a crappy salary for such an important job.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Mar 24, 2007 5:46 pm In Real Life, people can land important positions, sometimes for totally shady reasons, that they don't necessarily have the appropriate qualifications for. Even if they are, in theory, 'getting the job done', they can have "issues" to various levels of severity. And corruption is everywhere. Katayoku no Tenshi wrote: Or maybe just maybe she's the character that was chosen to be the operations director so she became it.

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Sat Mar 24, 2007 6:08 pm Reichu wrote: She certainly wasn't in the military.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Mar 24, 2007 7:15 pm ah that is true.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Sat Mar 24, 2007 7:52 pm Reichu wrote: Some conceptual art by Sadamoto shows her in fatigues (or something)

[]:             Quote Post by Katayoku no Tenshi » Sat Mar 24, 2007 8:46 pm EoE B_B's script wrote: Shigeru: (Handing pistol to Maya) Release the safety. Maya: I... I can't shoot. Shigeru: Of course you can! Just pretend this is training! Maya: But I wasn't shooting at people, then! Shigeru: Idiot! Shoot, or we're going to die!

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Mar 24, 2007 8:56 pm I think it's implied by Misato's titles, Captain->Major, and the titles of the bridge bunnies, that the personel in Nerv do operate on a Military ranking system. We also from above that in EoE that the Bridge bunnies all recieved basic gun training, again pointing to a Military structure at the organisation. But Nerv is a seperate organisation from the other branches of the UN, or Japan's, Military. It's certainly not a part of the (J)SSDF at all, with Fuyutsuki anxious in episode #18 to get Nerv rescue forces to Matsushiro, before any SSDF forces arrive. Nerv is very private about it's operations. Given this, as well as the scale of Nerv's budget, influence and importance, it could well be that instead of commandeering its personal from other branches of the military, Nerv literally trained, educated and commissioned all its own officers.

[]: Re: NERV positions and salaries             Quote Post by Rommel » Sun Mar 25, 2007 2:44 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: slothen wrote: Seems Misato is paid a crappy salary for such an important job.

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Sun Mar 25, 2007 3:05 pm on the other hand.. what are the PILOTS paid? :P

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Mar 25, 2007 3:13 pm slothen wrote: on the other hand.. are the PILOTS paid? :P
summary: []: NERV positions and salaries             Quote Post by slothen » Sat Mar 24, 2007 3:10 pm First of all, I'm posting this in the discussion forum, however depending on how the responses go it may be more appropriate in chit-chat. This topic is for the discussion of a few things: What kind of training/education is required to hold certain NERV positions? What kind of training/knowledge does Misato have that qualifies her as Operations
